title: Levelhead activation — sr_anchored filter wiring, ACTIVE_PHASE gate, per-index leg caps
acceptance_criteria: personality-filter Stage 1 (signal-type compatibility): entry_type 'sr_anchored' accepts the SR signal (sr_subtype='SR_REVERSAL') and rejects unrelated signal types — added alongside the existing fixed_time/momentum_exhaustion/any_signal mappings.

personality-filter Stage 4 (signal quality): for sr_anchored personalities, gate on params.sr_strength_threshold against signal.sr_strength instead of min_probability. min_probability gating is unchanged for non-sr_anchored personalities.

personality-router: introduce ACTIVE_PHASE env (default 1) and load personalities WHERE is_active=TRUE AND phase <= ACTIVE_PHASE (replacing the hardcoded phase <= 1). Document that ACTIVE_PHASE=2 activates Levelhead.
personality-router: REMOVE the hardcoded `signal.underlying as 'NIFTY'` cast — propagate the real underlying from the signal (D2/T-45 prerequisite).

Per-index leg cap (D2 Option A): the max_open_legs / open-position counting in Stage 2 counts open legs PER (personality, underlying) so each index is an independent 4-leg book — not a single cross-index total. The DailyState open-positions query is scoped by underlying.
Levelhead's existing cut_reenter (Reducer) management is reused as-is — no management code changes here.

Unit tests updated/added: sr_anchored Stage 1 acceptance + rejection, Stage 4 strength-threshold gate, ACTIVE_PHASE gating (phase=2 personality blocked at ACTIVE_PHASE=1, allowed at 2), per-underlying leg counting. tsc --noEmit clean; named exports.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision (esp. the per-underlying leg-count scoping and the Stage-4 strength gate)
